title: Shelf roller
abstract: Shelf roller (10) for conveying goods, comprising a plurality of shafts (3) arranged in parallel, wherein the shelf roller comprises:- at least one roller (2) arranged on each shaft (3), and adapted to rotate around the shaft (3);- a number of connection devices (1) arranged in pairs, each connection device having at least two holes (5), each hole being adapted for rotatable connection with a shaft (3); wherein the plurality of shafts (3) are arranged in groups, each group comprising at0least two adjacent shafts (3) installed in the holes (5) of a pair of connection devices (1) which are located one at each end of the adjacent shafts (3) of a group, and wherein each pair of connection devices (1) is hinged to a neighboring pair of connection devices (1) by the neighboring pairs being arranged on a common shaft(3) by end holes (5'), allowing the shelf roller (10) to be folded or rolled up.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Shelf roller (10) for conveying goods, comprising a plurality of shafts (3) arranged in parallel; characterized in that the shelf roller (10) further comprises: - at least one roller (2) arranged on each shaft (3), and adapted to rotate around the shaft (3); - a number of connection devices (1) arranged in pairs, each connection device having at least two holes (5), each hole being adapted for rotatable connection with a shaft (3); wherein the plurality of shafts (3) are arranged in groups, each group comprising at least two adjacent shafts (3) installed in the holes (5) of a pair of connection devices (1) which are located one at each end of the adjacent shafts (3) of a group, and wherein each pair of connection devices (1) is hinged to a neighboring pair of connection devices (1) by the neighboring pairs being arranged on a common shaft (3) by end holes (5â), allowing the shelf roller (10) to be folded or rolled up. 2. Shelf roller (10) according to claim 1 , wherein the connection devices (1) have a shape and size adapted to allow relative angular turning of neighboring pairs arranged on a common shaft (3). 3. Shelf roller (10) according to claim 2, wherein the holes (5) are arranged on each connection device (1) allowing the angular turning without end parts of the connection devices (1) colliding. 4. Shelf roller (10) according to claim 2 or 3, wherein end parts of the connection devices (1) are reciprocally formed/shaped allowing the angular turning. 5. Shelf roller (10) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the connection devices (1) have a shape and size adapted to limit the relative angular turning by at least parts of reciprocally formed end parts of the connection devices (1) colliding. 6. Shelf roller (10) according to claim 5, wherein the connection devices (1) have reciprocally formed end parts adapted to collide providing a shelf roller (10) 

 with a predefined form. 7. Shelf roller (10) according to claim 6, wherein the predefined form of the shelf roller (10) is mainly flat. 8. Shelf roller (10) according to any preceding claims, wherein the shafts (3) are either solid rods or hollow tubes having an outer profile which is circular or polygonal and wherein the inner diameter of the rollers (2) are at least slightly larger than the diameter of the outer circumference or circumcircle of the shaft (3). 9. Shelf roller (10) according to any of the preceding claims wherein neighboring pairs of connection devices (1) are arranged with an offset relative to each other on the shaft (3) in alternating directions along the length of the shelf roller creating a pattern of inner connection devices, being arranged closer to the central part of the shaft (3) and outer connection devices, being arranged closer to the end part of the shaft (3). 10. Shelf roller according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer diameter of the rollers (2) is such that the rollers (2) extend above the height of the connection devices (1). 11. Shelf roller according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the bottom sides of the connection devices (1) when rolled out provides a common base surface for placing the roller shelf on a flat support surface.
